summary: Guided filter (He et al.): filter an input under a guide's structure via a per-window local linear model q = a*I + b fitted by ridge regression (box radius, epsilon); edge-preserving, flat input preserved exactly.

inputs.name: input
inputs.doc: The Image or Field1 to filter (per channel).

inputs.name: guide
inputs.doc: The guide Image or Field1 whose structure the filter follows; a single-channel guide is broadcast across the input's channels.

outputs.name: output
outputs.doc: The guided-filtered result (same kind and extent as the input).

params.name: radius
params.doc: The box-window radius (the window side is 2*radius + 1); >= 1.

params.name: epsilon
params.doc: The ridge regularization bounding the linear coefficient; larger epsilon smooths flatter regions. Non-negative.

perceptual.reason: filter.guided is a closed-form local-linear-model filter verified by analytic properties (flat-input identity, flat-guide box-mean degeneracy, self-guided edge preservation, the per-window ridge fit against an independent box-statistics reference); there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
